steps.title: Use the data directly
steps.content: Use the exponential form $A(t)=a(b)^t$. The initial balance gives $a=35{,}800$. The 13-year balance gives $$67{,}506.24=35{,}800(b)^{13}.$$

steps.title: Find the growth multiplier
steps.content:
Divide by $35{,}800$ and take the 13th root:
$$b=\left(\frac{67{,}506.24}{35{,}800}\right)^{1/13}\approx1.05.$$
So the model is $$A(t)=35800(1.05)^t.$$

steps.title: Compare with the choices
steps.content: Choice A, $A \left(t\right)=35,800.00{ \left(1.05\right)}^{t}$, has both the correct starting value and the growth multiplier. The remaining alternative uses $0.05$, which would make the balance almost zero after 13 years. Choice A is correct.
